capabilities.label: Protected design and flowchart alignment
capabilities.scope: The protected Markdown design, HTML flowchart, JSON flowchart contract, and development docs describe the current target architecture.

capabilities.label: Source-side standard deliverable
capabilities.scope: The standard profile publishes source-side Markdown and assets when quality gates pass, preserves Markdown sources by using .cleaned.md, and records publish reports for published or blocked decisions.

capabilities.label: Conversion quality gate
capabilities.scope: Conversion quality checks, manifest checks, and validation for Canonical IR typed-node, source-span, transformation-ledger, and coverage-report evidence. The pre-clean gate reads complete route-wide IR semantics (relationships/assets/annotations) when the manifest declares them available and uses complete typed-node/source-span text quality evidence when coverage is complete. YouTube/media/image optional routes remain partial (Wave 4).
prohibitedClaims: all conversion routes have complete Canonical IR gate coverage

capabilities.label: Canonical IR contract
capabilities.scope: The worker emits a partial Canonical IR manifest plus validated canonical_ir/typed_nodes.json, canonical_ir/source_spans.json, and canonical_ir/transformation_ledger.json artifacts for heading, paragraph, list, table, code, quote, formula, figure, metadata, and transcript cue nodes. It also emits C2 content-safe canonical_ir/relationships.json, canonical_ir/assets.json, and canonical_ir/annotations.json artifacts for typed-node structure links, figure image references, and coverage warnings. SourceSpans cover converted Markdown line ranges for every typed node, strict evidence schema validation, structured-data text sources, and transcript cue timing when raw cue evidence is available. The SourceSpan schema validates route-native precision records when converters provide required native fields, including PDF bbox, DOCX run range, PPTX shape id, XLSX cell range, transcript cue id, and YouTube cue id precision. Coverage reports list missing native precision kinds instead of fabricating converter evidence, and relationships/assets/annotations gaps move to partial only when their artifacts contain records. The pre-clean conversion gate can use complete typed-node/source-span text evidence when coverage is complete. The standard Markdown render path can regenerate cleaned.md from Canonical IR typed-node text plus Clean View accepted change identity, while accepted patch entries use accepted in-memory cleanup block content and cleaning_patches.jsonl remains content-safe. Converter-native SourceSpan extraction ships for DOCX run-range, PPTX shape id, XLSX cell range, and PDF bbox via MinerU content_list (commits 6d9776e, f3727ae, bf80370, 2265d74); PDF Tier 1 (pymupdf4llm) text-layer bbox is a deferred gap because that lightweight engine does not expose bbox (pinned by 4b27d8c), and YouTube cue-id precision depends on the still-partial YouTube route. Route-wide relationship, asset, and quality-annotation semantics ship (commit 6d443ce). The pre-clean conversion gate consumes route-wide IR semantics when the manifest declares them available (commit 17e6154). Remaining target work: full Markdown renderer/profile coverage beyond the standard regeneration path (db5f39b), and universal fact-layer usage across every route. YouTube/media/image optional routes stay partial (Wave 4).
prohibitedClaims: Canonical IR is the complete shipped worker fact layer
Canonical IR Markdown regeneration covers every route and output profile

capabilities.label: Document type classification
capabilities.scope: The worker writes document_classification.json with conservative policy-use gating; the full target ClassificationPack and DocumentTypeSnapshot contract is not complete.
prohibitedClaims: DocumentTypeSnapshot is the complete shipped classification contract

capabilities.label: CleaningPolicySnapshot
capabilities.scope: The worker writes cleaning_policy_snapshot.json with selected policy input routes, active file paths and SHA-256 hashes, filtered accepted-rule fingerprints, active rule ids, dictionary ids, protection ids, disabled rule ids, conflict resolutions, preference selectors, compiler version, threshold summary, section hashes, and quality/run metadata references. Cache matching uses the policy hash after the document type is known. The snapshot records ids and hashes without copying private rule bodies, accepted-rule patterns, or source text.
prohibitedClaims: every shipped cleanup change is a guarded CleaningPatch

capabilities.label: CleaningPatch and Clean View
capabilities.scope: The worker writes accepted cleaning_patches.jsonl records for cleanup-stage block changes with block ids, change type, before/after safe metadata, rule ids, policy snapshot hash, source location hints, and text-changed status. The patch_quality_gate rejects unsafe candidate patches before rendering, restores rejected changes in memory, writes a safe cleaning_patch_gate.json summary, and writes content-safe rejected_patches.jsonl records for rejected patches. Clean View assembly writes a content-safe clean_view.json artifact from canonical_ir/typed_nodes.json plus accepted patch identity and renders cleaned.md through the assembled Clean View. DocumentCleaningGate writes content-safe document_cleaning_gate.json, blocks publication when Clean View evidence is invalid or incomplete, and surfaces rejected patch counts as warnings without blocking safe output.

capabilities.label: Single-source job status envelope
capabilities.scope: The worker envelope carries completed, completed_with_warnings, or failed status. Single-source prepare maps strict errors to failed, non-blocking warnings to completed_with_warnings, and clean successful runs to completed. TypeScript validates the required status field across success and failure envelopes.

capabilities.label: Proposal-first feedback learning
capabilities.scope: Feedback proposals, acceptance, rejection, public single-source selective rerun planning and execution, run-level Canonical IR manifest binding for selected runs (document_id, source identity, document type, policy snapshot hash), proposal risk notes, explicit owner confirmation status, scope-based dictionary suggestion thresholds, stronger body-text counterexamples, and failed-promotion-history blocking. Canonical IR node-level identity (canonical_node_ids) is now extracted from typed_nodes and recorded in the binding with node_identity_available=true when stable node_ids exist; execution-level selective rerun that filters the worker to specific node-ids remains future work (id_level_narrowing=false, run-level rerun).
prohibitedClaims: feedback learning proves every promoted rule against all future sources

capabilities.label: Batch, Playlist, and rerun
capabilities.scope: Batch behavior exists for local sources and writes a parent status manifest with evidence-backed rerun scope, source hashes, command defaults, and skipped unsupported visibility. Batch selective rerun can execute failed, pending, failed-and-pending, or recommended parent-manifest scopes, writes batch_rerun_manifest.json, and refuses missing or changed source files instead of claiming success. Explicit YouTube playlist input expands into bounded local .url child descriptors, reuses the subtitle-first YouTube route for each child, records source_collection metadata, and keeps per-video status visible in batch_manifest.json. Playlist rerun uses the same parent-manifest path and preserves source_collection plus child source_url evidence in batch_rerun_manifest.json. Single-source feedback selective rerun execution exists. Policy-affected batch targeting (run-evidence identity matching at the worker level) is implemented; Canonical IR id-level (node-id) narrowing remains future; the `policy_affected` rerun scope and its `affected_*` identity fields are now exposed through the CLI.
prohibitedClaims: Playlist processing is a verified shipped capability

capabilities.label: Local media transcript route
capabilities.scope: Local audio/video detection, external transcript routing, dependency failure reporting (ffmpeg/whisper missing), command evidence, and mocked golden transcript fixtures exist. Real local ASR dual-track manual acceptance evidence (zh fixture via qwen3-asr + en fixture via Whisper large-v3, transcript enters cleanup and final outputs, quality gates pass with 0 strict errors) is recorded; a reproducible version-controlled zh fixture now ships (python/tests/golden/formats/media/transcript_zh_90s.txt, qwen3-asr Qwen3-ASR-1.7B on a 90s sample); the route stays partial until deterministic or cross-sample ASR evidence supports verified promotion (see docs/development/asr-dual-track-acceptance.md).
prohibitedClaims: local media transcript route is verified with real ASR sample evidence

capabilities.label: YouTube URL route
capabilities.scope: Direct YouTube URLs, explicit video ids, and local .url descriptors can route through subtitle-first extraction. Accepted URL-shape evidence covers watch, mobile watch, youtu.be, shorts, and embed URLs. Media fallback is explicit, only runs when allow_youtube_media_fallback is true, downloads video/media through the yt-dlp Python package, then transcribes through the local media route. YouTube subtitle success reports preserve source URL, recorded-equivalent inventory evidence JSON, selected subtitle language, subtitle artifact path, transcript artifact path, and sanitized command evidence. Tests use recorded-equivalent subtitle inventory/report-contract coverage plus mocked yt-dlp subtitle inventory/download behavior, Python-library media download fallback, ffmpeg, Whisper behavior, and URL-shape contract tests; verified promotion still needs broader real-network, dependency, timeout, and transcript-quality evidence; a reproducible version-controlled YouTube subtitle fixture now ships (python/tests/golden/formats/youtube/subtitle_en_caQ2pfhoPcs.txt, video CAQ2pfhoPcs en auto subs via yt-dlp).
prohibitedClaims: YouTube input is a verified real-network capability
YouTube media fallback runs without explicit user request
